api: Datadog API v1 (https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/) — the connector's docs_url/base_url-targeted surface
docs: https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/ (canonical reference); OpenAPI spec cross-checked at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-go/master/.generator/schemas/v1/openapi.yaml, fetched 2026-07-04
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every one of the 235 documented Datadog API v1 operations (the exact surface published at docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/'s v1 section, cross-checked against DataDog's own published OpenAPI v1 spec) is enumerated below, each covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a specific reason. 12 streams (monitors/dashboards/dashboard_lists/users/slo/slo_corrections/downtimes/notebooks/organizations/hosts/synthetics_tests/synthetics_locations/synthetics_variables/api_keys/application_keys) and 20 writes now cover every practical GET-list/detail resource and every dialect-expressible mutation this API version supports. The Datadog v2 API (a separate, vastly larger ~1383-operation surface spanning entirely distinct product areas — Security Monitoring, Cloud Cost Management, Case Management, LLM Observability, Incidents, Teams, Static Analysis, Status Pages, Feature Flags, App Builder, On-Call, Org Groups/Roles/Service Accounts identity administration, and dozens more — none of which this connector's docs_url/base_url ever targeted) is out of scope for this pass; see docs.md's Known limits for the v1-vs-v2 scope-boundary rationale (the same reasoning applied to the dbt bundle's v2-vs-v3 boundary in this same wave).

rotates the AWS integration's cross-account trust external id; integration-credential management, not connector data
AWS Logs integration wiring (Lambda ARN), not connector data
AWS Logs integration wiring (Lambda ARN), not connector data
AWS Logs integration wiring (Lambda ARN), not connector data
async permission-check utility for AWS Logs integration wiring, not a data resource
static catalog of AWS-log-ready service names, not account data
AWS Logs integration wiring (enable log collection per service), not connector data
async permission-check utility for AWS Logs integration wiring, not a data resource
credential validation is already exercised by this bundle's own check.path (GET /api/v1/dashboard); an equivalent auth-only ping, not an independent data resource
cloud-provider integration credential management (Azure tenant/client secret), not connector data
cloud-provider integration credential management (Azure tenant/client secret), not connector data
cloud-provider integration credential management (Azure tenant/client secret), not connector data

single-dashboard-list detail is a strict subset of the dashboard_lists stream's already-read per-list fields

bulk-deletes MULTIPLE dashboards by id list in one call; delete_dashboard already covers the single-dashboard case with the same real effect, per-item risk framing, and idempotent-delete semantics this dialect models well; the bulk variant's higher blast radius has no corresponding single-call safety rail in this dialect

restores previously soft-deleted dashboards by id list within a limited recovery window; a narrow admin-recovery action with no corresponding read state this bundle tracks (deleted dashboards are absent from the dashboards stream, so there is nothing to select a record from for this action)
creates a PUBLIC, unauthenticated shareable dashboard URL; a distinct higher-risk sharing feature from ordinary dashboard CRUD, not implemented in this pass
manages public-dashboard-sharing revocation; see the public-dashboard creation exclusion above
reads a public-dashboard-sharing configuration; see the public-dashboard creation exclusion above
mutates a public-dashboard-sharing configuration; see the public-dashboard creation exclusion above
manages public-dashboard email invitations; see the public-dashboard creation exclusion above
reads public-dashboard email invitations; see the public-dashboard creation exclusion above
sends a public-dashboard email invitation; see the public-dashboard creation exclusion above

single-dashboard detail (full widget definitions) is a strict superset only in widget-body detail of the dashboards stream's own per-record fields; the catalog-level dashboards stream already reads every dashboard's identity/metadata, and reading each dashboard's full widget body individually is a per-parent-id fan-out this bundle does not need for catalog-level sync parity
cancels every downtime matching a scope string in one call; cancel_downtime already covers the single-downtime-by-id case with the same real effect and a clearer per-record risk framing
single-downtime detail is a strict subset of the downtimes stream's already-read per-record fields
per-monitor downtime lookup is a filtered view of the same records the downtimes stream already reads in full (each downtime record carries its own monitor_id/monitor_tags)

the real endpoint requires BOTH start and end as mandatory POSIX-timestamp query parameters; end has no fixed/derivable value (the real use is always "now") and the engine's template Vars environment has no resolvable "current time" reference (grep confirms no time.Now()-equivalent anywhere in engine/{interpolate,read}.go, the same gap documented for datascope's windowed streams) — ENGINE_GAP, not a scoping choice
single-event detail depends on first reading the events list, which is itself excluded above (ENGINE_GAP: no resolvable current-time reference for the required end parameter)
cloud-provider integration credential management (GCP service account key), not connector data
cloud-provider integration credential management (GCP service account key), not connector data
cloud-provider integration credential management (GCP service account key), not connector data
cloud-provider integration credential management (GCP service account key), not connector data

mutes ALL monitors for a host by hostname (a host-scoped alert-suppression action with its own recovery-timeout semantics), a materially different shape from create_downtime's scope-list model; not implemented this pass
reverses the host-mute action above; excluded for the same reason (action not implemented)
a bare active/total host count already derivable by counting the hosts stream's own records; no independent record shape
publishes Datadog's own outbound IP address ranges for firewall allow-listing (a static, non-account-scoped reference document), not Datadog account/organization data
single-API-key detail is a strict subset of the api_keys stream's already-read per-record fields

creates an application key SCOPED TO THE CALLING USER (no name-only account-level equivalent to api_key's create); mints a new personal credential on behalf of whichever identity the connector authenticates as, an identity-management action rather than an account-data mutation
revokes a personal application key; identity-management action, not an account-data mutation
single-application-key detail is a strict subset of the application_keys stream's already-read per-record fields
renames a personal application key; identity-management action, not an account-data mutation

full-text log SEARCH (a query surface with its own filter/facet grammar and huge result volumes), a fundamentally different shape from this bundle's catalog/config-resource streams; log data ingestion/search is out of scope for this configuration-focused connector

log-ingestion endpoint (accepts arbitrary externally-supplied log payloads for injection into the organization's log pipeline); a data-ingestion write with no corresponding read/catalog shape, out of scope for a configuration-focused connector

log-pipeline administration surface (processing-pipeline definitions); out of scope for this pass, see Known limits
metrics-ingestion endpoint (submits distribution-point metric payloads); a telemetry write with no corresponding catalog read shape, out of scope for a configuration-focused connector

lists every ACTIVE METRIC NAME reporting in the last 24h — a potentially enormous, high-cardinality, constantly-changing set (unlike this bundle's bounded config-resource streams: monitors, dashboards, SLOs, etc.); metrics catalog/timeseries data is a fundamentally different product surface (Datadog's dedicated Metrics product), out of scope for this configuration-focused connector
single-metric metadata depends on first enumerating metric names, which is itself excluded above
mutates a single metric's metadata (unit, description); depends on the excluded metrics-catalog surface above

queries TIMESERIES METRIC DATA POINTS (raw numeric telemetry over a time range) — an analytics/query surface, not a catalog resource; fundamentally different from this bundle's bounded config-resource streams
full-text search across metrics/tags/hosts; a search surface, not an independent catalog resource, and depends on the excluded metrics-catalog surface above
metrics-ingestion endpoint (submits metric data points); a telemetry write with no corresponding catalog read shape, out of scope for a configuration-focused connector

a pre-flight eligibility check (whether given monitor ids can be safely deleted), not a data resource; delete_monitor's own request/response already reports the real outcome of an actual delete attempt
monitor GROUP search (multi-alert monitor group state), a distinct, more specialized query surface than the monitors stream's own catalog listing
monitor search-by-query is a filtered view over the same records the monitors stream already reads in full
validates a monitor definition WITHOUT creating it (a dry-run utility), not a data mutation; create_monitor's own request already validates server-side on the real create call
single-monitor detail is a strict subset of the monitors stream's already-read per-record fields
validates an existing monitor's current definition without mutating it (a dry-run utility), not a data mutation

single-notebook detail (full cell content) is a strict superset only in cell-body detail of the notebooks stream's own per-record catalog fields; reading every notebook's full cell content individually is a per-parent-id fan-out this bundle does not need for catalog-level sync parity
creates a new CHILD ORGANIZATION under a multi-org account; a rare account-hierarchy administration action, not ordinary connector data
single-organization detail is a strict subset of the organizations stream's already-read per-record fields
mutates organization-level billing/SAML/security settings; account-admin scope, not ordinary connector data
irreversibly spins off/downgrades a child organization's subscription tier; a rare, high-impact account-hierarchy action outside this connector's scope
uploads SAML identity-provider metadata for the organization; security/identity-administration action, not connector data
third-party integration wiring (PagerDuty service objects), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (PagerDuty service objects), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (PagerDuty service objects), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (PagerDuty service objects), not connector data

Security Monitoring is a separate, dedicated Datadog product surface (signal triage workflow, mostly v2-only) with no corresponding read/catalog stream in this bundle; out of scope for this configuration-focused connector — see docs.md's Known limits for the v1-vs-v2 scope boundary
Security Monitoring triage action; see the add_to_incident exclusion above
Security Monitoring triage action; see the add_to_incident exclusion above
service-check-ingestion endpoint (submits a check-status telemetry event); a telemetry write with no corresponding catalog read shape, out of scope for a configuration-focused connector
creates an SLO correction (excludes a time window from an SLO's error-budget calculation); no dialect-expressible write action implemented this pass, a candidate for a future increment
deletes an SLO correction; see the create exclusion above
single-SLO-correction detail is a strict subset of the slo_corrections stream's already-read per-record fields
updates an SLO correction; see the create exclusion above
bulk-deletes SLO TIMEFRAMES across multiple SLO ids in one call; delete_slo already covers the single-SLO-by-id case with the same real effect and a clearer per-record risk framing
a pre-flight eligibility check (whether given SLO ids can be safely deleted), not a data resource
SLO search-by-query is a filtered view over the same records the slo stream already reads in full
single-SLO detail is a strict subset of the slo stream's already-read per-record fields
per-SLO correction lookup is a filtered view of the same records the slo_corrections stream already reads in full (each correction record carries its own slo_id)
SLO error-budget/status HISTORY (a timeseries-shaped analytics resource keyed by time range), a fundamentally different shape from this bundle's bounded config-resource streams
third-party integration wiring (Slack channel routing), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (Slack channel routing), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (Slack channel routing), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (Slack channel routing), not connector data
third-party integration wiring (Slack channel routing), not connector data
renders and returns a graph image (PNG) snapshot URL for a metric query; an image-rendering utility, not a structured JSON list/detail record
CI/CD-triggered test-batch run RESULTS (a run-history resource keyed by an opaque batch id from a CI pipeline invocation), not a catalog resource this bundle's synthetics_tests stream already covers
provisions a customer-hosted Synthetics private-location worker; infrastructure-provisioning action, not ordinary connector data
de-provisions a customer-hosted Synthetics private-location worker; infrastructure action, not ordinary connector data
reads a customer-hosted Synthetics private-location worker's config (may include connection secrets); infrastructure-scoped, not ordinary connector data
mutates a customer-hosted Synthetics private-location worker's config; infrastructure action, not ordinary connector data
the account's default-location LIST is a strict subset of the synthetics_locations stream's already-read location catalog
single-API-test detail is a strict subset of the synthetics_tests stream's already-read per-record fields

Browser tests require a recorded step sequence authored through Datadog's own visual test recorder; there is no practical way to construct one via a declarative record body, unlike API tests' plain HTTP-request config — no dialect-expressible write for this test type
single-browser-test detail is a strict subset of the synthetics_tests stream's already-read per-record fields
mutates a browser test's recorded step sequence; see the browser-test-creation exclusion above
browser-test RUN RESULTS (a run-history/results resource with its own pass/fail timeline), a fundamentally different shape from the synthetics_tests catalog stream
single browser-test run result; see the results-list exclusion above

bulk-deletes MULTIPLE Synthetics tests by id list in one call; no corresponding single-test delete write is implemented this pass because delete affects the actively-scheduled probe (a live-monitoring side effect not yet modeled for this resource, unlike create/update)
Mobile tests require a recorded step sequence authored through Datadog's mobile app testing tooling; same shape gap as browser tests above, no dialect-expressible write
single-mobile-test detail is a strict subset of the synthetics_tests stream's already-read per-record fields
mutates a mobile test's recorded step sequence; see the mobile-test-creation exclusion above
Synthetics test search-by-query is a filtered view over the same records the synthetics_tests stream already reads in full

triggers an on-demand, out-of-schedule Synthetics test run; a run-control action for a resource whose catalog (not run-history) is this bundle's scope, no dialect-expressible write implemented this pass
triggers Synthetics test runs specifically from a CI/CD pipeline context (extra CI-provenance metadata in the request); see the generic trigger exclusion above
fetches aggregate UPTIME METRICS for multiple tests over a time range; an analytics/metrics resource, not a catalog resource
generic single-test detail (any test type) is a strict subset of the synthetics_tests stream's already-read per-record fields

generic partial-update-by-type endpoint; update_synthetics_api_test already covers the API-test case this bundle models, and browser/mobile test mutation is out of scope per the type-specific exclusions above
API-test RUN RESULTS (a run-history/results resource with its own pass/fail timeline), a fundamentally different shape from the synthetics_tests catalog stream
single API-test run result; see the results-list exclusion above
pauses/resumes a test's ACTIVE PROBING SCHEDULE; a run-control toggle for a resource whose catalog (not schedule-state control) is this bundle's current write scope

creates a Synthetics global variable; some variable values are marked secure/secret-shaped at the API's own discretion (is_fido/is_totp/secure flags), and this bundle does not yet model a write-only-credential write action for this resource type, unlike SSH-tunnel's documented precedent in the dbt bundle — candidate for a future increment
deletes a Synthetics global variable; see the create exclusion above
single-variable detail is a strict subset of the synthetics_variables stream's already-read per-record fields
updates a Synthetics global variable; see the create exclusion above

the real response is a map of TAG NAME to an ARRAY OF HOSTNAMES (`{"tags": {"<tag>": ["host1", "host2"]}}`) — an object whose VALUES are arrays, not objects; the engine's records.keyed_object flag explodes a keyed object's OBJECT-valued entries into records and does not apply when values are arrays, and there is no other declarative shape for a tag-to-hostnames map — ENGINE_GAP, not a scoping choice
mutates tags for a single host; depends on the excluded host-tags catalog surface above for any meaningful record-shaped read/write pairing
single-host tag lookup is a scalar array response (list of tag strings for one host), a narrower view of the same excluded host-tags surface above; no independent catalog shape
adds tags to a single host; see the host-tags catalog exclusion above
replaces tags on a single host; see the host-tags catalog exclusion above
